A1-EDU-ATTEND.title: School attendance & completion in T&T (MICS 2022)
A1-EDU-ATTEND.caption: Completion drops from 99% (primary) to 85% (upper secondary)—and attendance gaps widen by sex and income.

A1-EDU-DROPOUT.title: Secondary school dropouts (Gov’t & Gov’t‐Assisted, 2019/20)
A1-EDU-DROPOUT.caption: In 2019/20, 508 students were recorded as dropouts; 61% were male.
A1-EDU-DROPOUT.source: Ministry of Education, Education Statistics Digest (2019/2020).

A1-LABOUR-YOUTH.title: Youth unemployment (15–24) vs overall (2025 Q3)
A1-LABOUR-YOUTH.caption: Youth unemployment rises above the national rate—especially for young women in 2025 Q3.
A1-LABOUR-YOUTH.source: Central Statistical Office, Continuous Sample Survey of Population (CSSP).

A2-YTRC-DRUG.title: Young offenders remanded for drug offences (YTRC, 2018–2022)
A2-YTRC-DRUG.caption: The DIN‐TT report shows remands falling from 24 (2018) to 1 (2022) for drug offences.
A2-YTRC-DRUG.source: Ministry of National Security (DIN‐TT Annual Report 2022; YTRC data).

A2-CRIME-PIPELINE.title: Crime‐2024: reports → prosecutions → convictions
A2-CRIME-PIPELINE.caption: In 2024, the system recorded a large drop from reports to convictions—helpful context for “nothing seen, nothing said.”

A2-CONVICT-AGE.title: Convictions by age group (drug & drug‐related, 2018–2022)
A2-CONVICT-AGE.caption: Over 2018–2022, DIN‐TT reports 3,650 drug/drug‐related convictions, 98% male.
A2-CONVICT-AGE.source: Ministry of National Security (DIN‐TT Annual Report 2022; Prison Service data).
